title: Family Finance Command Deck
subtitle: Executive view of household cash, commitments, debt, and long-term capital.

dataQuality.label: Actuals live
dataQuality.detail: May 2026 actuals extracted from ABSA, Standard Bank, and Discovery statements. Balances as at 30 May 2026.

executiveSummary: May 2026: household health reads critical. Income R65,619 versus outflows approximately R63,800, leaving a thin surplus of R1,819. Liquid cash of R45,792 covers less than one month of outflows. A potential forced relocation requires R45,000 new deposit against R44,000 held with current landlord. Vehicle load at 21% of income. Savings rate zero. With corrected Audi settlement value, net worth is now R48,259.

focusItems.title: Relocation capital gap
focusItems.detail: Safety concerns at Zeeland Crescent may require a move. New deposit: R45,000 needed. Current deposit held: R44,000. Net shortfall: R1,000 plus moving costs. Cash position cannot absorb this without drawing on reserves.

focusItems.title: Cash is near zero
focusItems.detail: ABSA cleared to R87. SBSA at R22,163 only after a R12,000 same-day transfer. Discovery main account is the primary buffer at R23,539. Combined liquid cash is R45,792 — less than one month of outflows.

focusItems.title: New insurance line R4,680
focusItems.detail: 1 Life Med 9 Zeeland added in April at R4,680/month. Confirm whether this replaces or adds to existing cover. If additive, it pushes total insurance above R10,000/month.

focusItems.title: Restart saving
focusItems.detail: No formal saving is occurring. Balance sheet is not strengthening. Even R2,000/month would begin to change the trajectory.

watchlistRows.reason: Combined liquid cash of R45,792 covers less than one month of outflows.
watchlistRows.action: Do not touch reserves. Prioritise rebuilding transaction cash next payday.

watchlistRows.reason: Safety concerns at Zeeland Crescent may force a move. New deposit required before landlord releases current one.
watchlistRows.action: Quantify timeline. Identify bridge funding source — reserves are the only option currently.

watchlistRows.reason: Two vehicle finance payments consuming R10,992/month before fuel.
watchlistRows.action: Decision on vehicle strategy still outstanding.

watchlistRows.reason: No savings allocated for the fourth consecutive month.
watchlistRows.action: Begin R2,000/month minimum once relocation risk is resolved.

watchlistRows.reason: Account cleared after salary disbursements. All funds routed out immediately.
watchlistRows.action: Maintain at minimum R2,000 float at all times.

decisionRows.title: Relocation decision
decisionRows.detail: Safety concerns are real. If moving, need R45,000 new deposit while awaiting return of R44,000 from current landlord. Net cash gap: approximately R1,000 plus moving costs (estimate R5,000-R10,000). Total exposure: up to R11,000 above the deposit. Only viable funding source currently is Discovery reserves.

decisionRows.title: Vehicle strategy
decisionRows.detail: BMW + Audi finance + fuel = approximately R15,500/month. Selling one vehicle would free up approximately R5,000-R7,000/month immediately and reduce debt by R153,000-R269,000.

decisionRows.title: Restart monthly saving
decisionRows.detail: Once relocation is settled, begin R2,000/month minimum recurring transfer to reserve. Track via dashboard.

cashAccounts.notes: R12,000 transferred in from Munya today.
Medical saver — earmarked for health costs.

investmentRows.notes: Recoverable on exit. May be needed for relocation bridge.

debtHighlights.notes: 2014 Audi A6 2.0 TDi. 43 months outstanding. Settlement R151,358.

alerts.title: JUNE PAYDAYS NEEDED URGENTLY
alerts.detail: BMW finance R6,299 and school fees R4,135 debit in the first 3 days of June. Current SBSA balance R22,163. Munya must transfer before 1 June.

alerts.title: Relocation capital planning
alerts.detail: If the household moves, R45,000 new deposit is required. Current deposit of R44,000 held by landlord will not be returned until handover. Bridge gap must be planned from reserves.
